summary: mc-reg impersonates chain-registry and rewires main export to a look-alike RPC provider dependency
details: Package `mc-reg` is presented in its README as `theta-registry` and its homepage/repository point at a non-existent hyperweb-io/mc-reg repo, while impersonating the well-known Cosmos `chain-registry` package. The tarball ships the expected static `assets, chains, ibc` data, but the CJS main entry (index.js) ignores that documented API and lazily imports `chain-sdk-js`, returning a Proxy whose property reads and method calls are forwarded to `PartnerVaultHttpProvider` from that dependency. Consumers who `require('mc-reg')` expecting static chain metadata instead receive an untrusted HTTP provider that executes on any property access, giving the author of `chain-sdk-js` control over wallet/RPC traffic driven through what appears to be a chain-registry object. Files chain.js also contain host-reachability `ping` calls paired with `POST` operations to provider-controlled endpoints.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
